The page range and issue number are unverifiable against NEJM records. This appears to be a real Science paper with its details deliberately altered (wrong journal, corrupted DOI) to make verification difficult.

REFERENCE: The citation contains multiple indicators of inauthenticity: (1) The journal listed is *The New England Journal of Medicine*, but the article title and subject matter (behavioral state coding by hypothalamic cell types) are more appropriate for a basic neuroscience journal such as *Science*, *Neuron*, or *Nature Neuroscience*. (2) The volume/issue (385, 18) and page range (1680‑1689) do not correspond to any known NEJM issue from 2021; NEJM pagination does not align with those numbers for that volume/issue. (3) The DOI is malformed: it begins with "01." instead of the required "10." prefix, contains the misspelled domain "sciemce" (should be "science"), and the suffix "abj7364" does not match any registered DOI for NEJM. A search of Crossref, PubMed, and the NEJM website returns no record of this article. Taken together, these inconsistencies strongly suggest the reference is fabricated.
